title: Marshmallow leaf infusion for cough
subtitle: A cold-steep soothing herb for a tickling cough
excerpt: Dried marshmallow leaf cold-steeped in water to draw out its soothing gel. Drunk warm for a dry, persistent tickling cough. The cold infusion preserves more soothing gel than a hot steep.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve (the early 20th-century botanical writer), A Modern Herbal, marshmallow entry: the soothing herb leaf and root preparations for coughs and irritated mucous membranes. the herbal reference on Althaeae folium, the traditional use for dry irritating cough and sore throat.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dried marshmallow leaf stores 12 months in a sealed tin, cool and dark. The cold infusion can be prepared 30 minutes to 2 hours before needed.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation made by steeping herb in cold or room-temperature water rather than hot water. Used for mucilage-rich herbs, where cold water extracts more of the mucilage than hot water does.

content.text: Marshmallow leaf (Althaea officinalis) is one of the most gel-forming herbs in the western tradition. The leaf contains abundant polysaccharide soothing gel that dissolves readily in water and coats the throat and airways as it is swallowed. The key preparation detail is the water temperature: a

(room-temperature or cold water, 30-60 minutes' steeping) extracts more soothing gel than a hot steep, because heat begins to break down the soothing gel polysaccharides. Prepare in the morning for use through the day, or 30 minutes before needed. The preparation is one of the safest in herbal medicine and suits children and elderly readers equally.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Drug absorption. The soothing gel coats the gut lining and can slow the absorption of oral medication taken at the same time. Take marshmallow leaf preparations at least 2 hours apart from any prescription drug.

Marshmallow leaf has no other significant safety concerns at standard doses. It is among the safest herbs in domestic use.

Coughs that need medical attention. A cough with blood-streaked mucus, significant breathlessness, a cough with stridor, a cough in an infant under 12 months, or a persistent cough of more than 3 weeks needs medical assessment.

items.prepNote: dried marshmallow leaf or root; leaf for this cold-infusion method
cold or room-temperature; do not use hot water for this preparation

content.text: Combine the dried marshmallow and cold water in a teapot or covered jug. Stir gently. Leave to steep at room temperature for 30 minutes, or in the fridge for up to 4 hours if preparing ahead. The liquid will become slightly viscous, noticeably more slippery than plain water. Strain through a sieve into a cup. Warm gently on the hob to a comfortable drinking temperature (do not boil). Drink slowly to maximise contact with the throat and airways.

One cup (250 ml, containing 3 g dried leaf/root) up to four times a day, taken at least 2 hours away from any prescription medication. Use for the duration of an acute episode (typically 5-7 days). If the cough persists beyond 2 weeks, seek medical advice. Suitable for children over 12; for younger children, consult a herbalist for age-appropriate dosing.
The cold infusion keeps 24 hours in the fridge in a sealed jar. Reheat each cup before drinking. Dried marshmallow stores 12 months in a sealed tin, cool and dark.
When not to use this remedy
Within 2 hours of prescription medication. Coughs with blood, severe breathlessness, significant fever, or stridor need medical assessment.
